decks.notes: This deck focuses on quickly assembling the Allosaurus Combo by using cards like Eldritch Evolution, Neoform, and Summoner's Pact to tutor for Allosaurus Rider and other combo pieces. The deck also includes cards like Nourishing Shoal and Planar Genesis for additional combo potential and protection. By utilizing powerful ramp strategies and cheating in high-cost creatures like Ghalta, Stampede Tyrant and Griselbrand, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents and win through a quick, explosive combo finish. The inclusion of cards like Endurance and Veil of Summer also helps to protect against disruption and control strategies.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through powerful spells and utilizing the UrzaTron lands to ramp up mana quickly. It includes a mix of counterspells, removal, and powerful threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Ugin, the Spirit Dragon. The goal is to disrupt the opponent's strategy while building towards casting big threats that can quickly dominate the game. The deck also includes cards like Stock Up and Portent of Calamity to help draw cards and filter through the deck for key pieces.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature plays such as Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Seasoned Pyromancer, supported by burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Thraben Charm. The deck aims to quickly deal damage to the opponent while controlling the board with cards like Goblin Bombardment and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah. With a mix of creatures and spells, this deck has the potential to overwhelm opponents early in the game and secure a fast victory. Additionally, cards like Blood Moon and Ranger-Captain of Eos provide additional utility and disruption against opponents.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying artifacts and creatures to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays. Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Mox Opal allow for quick mana acceleration, while cards like Metallic Rebuke and Unholy Heat disrupt the opponent's game plan. Urza's Saga provides a strong recurring threat, and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student offers card advantage and utility. It also includes interaction pieces like Repeal and Pithing Needle for flexibility in handling various threats. Overall, the deck aims to quickly establish board presence and pressure the opponent to win the game efficiently.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creatures and burn spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. It utilizes cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to apply early pressure, while also including cards like Goblin Bombardment and Galvanic Discharge for additional reach. The deck also includes creatures like Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury for a powerful late-game threat. The inclusion of cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory provide additional ways to buff and protect your creatures, making it a fast-paced and versatile aggro deck.

The deck aims to create a powerful storm of spells by generating mana quickly with cards like Pyretic Ritual and Ruby Medallion. It uses a combination of draw spells like Reckless Impulse and Ral, Monsoon Mage's abilities to dig for key pieces like Past in Flames and Grapeshot. By casting multiple spells in a single turn, the deck can deal a large amount of damage to the opponent in a single burst. The inclusion of Wish allows for additional flexibility and options depending on the situation.

This deck focuses on aggressive creatures and disruption spells to control the game and disrupt the opponent's strategy. It includes powerful creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phage, Titan of Fire's Fury, along with cards like Quantum Riddler and Solitude for removal and protection. The goal is to apply pressure early, disrupt the opponent's game plan, and close out the game quickly before they have a chance to stabilize. The inclusion of Teferi, Time Raveler and other disruptive spells further adds to the deck's ability to control the game and dictate the pace.

The deck focuses on generating a large amount of mana quickly through rituals and medallions to cast spells and activate abilities in order to storm off and win the game in one explosive turn. The deck also utilizes card draw and selection to find combo pieces and maintain momentum, with the combo pieces being Ral, Monsoon Mage and Wrenn's Resolve. The deck also includes ways to protect itself and disrupt the opponent, making it a high-risk, high-reward strategy that requires precise sequencing and timing to execute effectively.

The deck focuses on using cards that can manipulate the graveyard and cycling cards to quickly fill it up. This allows the player to cast Living End, a powerful sorcery that returns all creatures from both players' graveyards to the battlefield, while getting rid of opponents' creatures. The deck also includes some control elements with counter spells and disruptive creatures to help protect the combo. Overall, the goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a large number of creatures and disrupt their game plan.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creatures and burn spells to quickly overwhelm opponents. The deck is designed to apply pressure early in the game with efficient creatures like Seasoned Pyromancer and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer, while also utilizing burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to finish off opponents. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury provide additional threats and ways to generate advantage. The deck also includes disruption in the form of Ranger-Captain of Eos and Thraben Charm to control the pace of the game and secure victory.

The main focus of this Modern deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with cards like Faithless Looting and Burning Inquiry to cheat out powerful creatures like Vengevine and Hollow One. By utilizing cards like Hedron Crab and Marauding Mako to mill cards, the deck can quickly enable its graveyard interactions. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Ox of Agonas and Detective's Phoenix for extra card draw and recursion. Ultimately, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and graveyard shenanigans to secure a quick victory.

This deck focuses on aggressive gameplay with early creature drops and controlling the board with burn spells. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast-paced offense, utilizing efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury, while also having access to removal like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to help clear the way. The deck also features some disruptive elements such as Blood Moon and Ranger-Captain of Eos to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The overall strategy is to apply pressure and quickly close out the game before the opponent can stabilize.

The deck focuses on generating mana quickly through rituals and medallions to cast Ral, Monsoon Mage and combo off with spells like Strike It Rich and Past in Flames. The goal is to generate a large storm count and finish the opponent off with a massive Grapeshot. The deck utilizes resources like Wish and Valakut Awakening to ensure consistency in drawing the combo pieces. The key strategy is to set up the perfect storm turn to overwhelm the opponent and win in one explosive turn.

The primary focus of this deck is to quickly establish a strong board presence using aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd, while disrupting your opponent's game plan with cards like Solitude and Consign to Memory. By combining elements of aggro and control, this deck aims to apply pressure on the opponent while maintaining a level of disruption to ensure victory. The inclusion of powerful creatures and versatile removal spells helps to create a dynamic and efficient playstyle that can overwhelm opponents in quick succession.

The deck focuses on aggressive creatures and disruption to keep the opponent on their toes. It utilizes powerful creatures like Phelia and Phlage to apply pressure while using cards like Consign to Memory and Galvanic Discharge to control the board. It also includes powerful removal spells like Solitude and Prismatic Ending to deal with threats. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while quickly closing out the game with its aggressive creatures. By combining strong creatures with disruption, it aims to outpace and outmaneuver the opponent to secure victory.

The main focus of this deck is to establish control over the game through the efficient ramping and powerful threats provided by the UrzaTron lands. By utilizing cards like Karn, the Great Creator, Ugin, the Eye of the Storms, and Thought-Knot Seer, the deck can disrupt opponents' strategies while also presenting a fast clock with Eldrazi creatures. The inclusion of cards like Devourer of Destiny and Kozilek's Command adds versatility and resilience to the deck's game plan, making it a formidable force in the Modern format.
